stories.title: State officials may decide Mason-area data center instead of locals
stories.dek: A proposal near Mason could bypass township review and go straight to state regulators. Ingham County residents fear losing input on power use and tax deals. The shift raises questions about local control over big industrial projects.

stories.title: York Township schedules hearing on data center moratorium
stories.dek: Residents will weigh a temporary ban after similar pauses passed in nearby Adrian and other towns. The hearing comes as Washtenaw County sees rising opposition to large server farms. People want time to assess impacts on roads, water and electricity rates.

stories.title: Hundreds pack hearing as Garfield Township pushes data-center moratorium
stories.dek: Grand Traverse County planners recommended a temporary ban after residents filled the room to protest power lines and industrial noise near homes and farms. The proposed rules would give the township time to study water and electric-grid limits before any large project moves forward. Locals 30 minutes away are tracking the vote because any approved campus could affect regional electric rates.

stories.title: Michigan GOP candidates split on taking state money for AI data centers
stories.dek: During a primary debate, hopefuls for governor were asked whether they would accept federal or state incentives to land hyperscale AI projects. Answers ranged from full-throated support to calls for stricter local control. The exchange shows data centers are now a live campaign issue.

stories.title: Mason recall drive keeps pressure on mayor over data center fight
stories.dek: Petition organizers continue collecting signatures to remove the mayor after disputes over a proposed data center in the Ingham County city. The effort shows how quickly local data center fights can turn into direct challenges to elected officials. Voters will decide whether the mayor stays if enough valid signatures are gathered.

stories.title: Adrian bars new data center proposals for the next year
stories.dek: City leaders approved a 12-month pause to give themselves time to write permanent rules before more projects arrive. The move follows resident pushback over power demand and land use in the small Lenawee County community. No new applications will be accepted while the moratorium is in effect.

stories.title: Oracle asks Saline Twp to skip 12 years of taxes on $43B campus
stories.dek: The township board is set to decide whether to grant a long tax break for one of the largest data center investments ever proposed in Michigan. Residents would forgo millions in property tax revenue while the project draws heavily on local power and water. The vote will show how much the community is willing to give up to land the facility.

stories.title: Girard Twp green-lights data center rules while Coldwater drafts its own
stories.dek: Two southwest Michigan townships are moving at different speeds to set limits on future data center projects. Girard has already adopted standards; Coldwater is just starting the drafting process. The differing approaches will shape where large facilities can locate and what they must provide in return.

stories.title: El-Sayed outlines strict conditions for data centers in Michigan Senate debate
stories.dek: Democratic candidate Abdul El-Sayed called for union labor requirements, no utility rate hikes, water protections, and enforceable community benefits agreements during the first one-on-one debate with Haley Stevens.

stories.title: Resident notes scale of Saline data center during drive
stories.dek: A Michigan resident described the Saline data center as rivaling Michigan International Speedway in size while passing the site.

stories.title: Democratic Senate candidates clash over data centers in latest debate
stories.dek: U.S. Rep. Haley Stevens and Abdul El-Sayed offered contrasting views on data center development during the Michigan U.S. Senate debate, with clips highlighting affordability concerns and corporate influence.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Regular meeting — data center zoning on the radar
Open meeting — oversees large-load electric agreements
